description_long: This object is a small terracotta pot filled with dark soil and containing a vibrant arrangement of three distinct cacti. The most prominent cactus features a bright red, globular top with white speckles, likely a grafted Gymnocalycium mihanovichii (moon cactus). Surrounding it are two other cacti: one with a dense cluster of long, sharp spines and another with a bumpy, green surface and short, white spines. The pot itself has a slightly rough, unglazed texture and a classic rounded lip. The soil is dark and granular, visible between the cacti. The overall composition is compact and visually striking, suitable for decorative use in urban environments.
description: A small terracotta pot holds three visually distinct cacti, including a red-topped moon cactus and two spiny green varieties, set in dark soil.
description_view_0: Front view shows the red-topped cactus centered, with the spiny and bumpy cacti clearly visible on either side.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the bumpy, white-spined cactus in the foreground, with the red-topped cactus slightly turned.
description_view_2: The red-topped cactus is most prominent, with the bumpy cactus to the left and the spiny cactus partially obscured.
description_view_3: The spiny cactus is in the foreground, with the red-topped cactus to the left and the bumpy cactus mostly hidden.
attribute_plant_species: cactus (including Gymnocalycium mihanovichii, and two other cacti)
